description: Architectural change: resolve repo: operations inline at their position in the declaration sequence, not eagerly in Phase 1. This is the change that fully fixes the reported bug (upstream auto-merges executing at the right point relative to consumer filters).
blocked_on: context/phase-0-spec-discovery.json (needs Q1 decision on repo-in-sequence semantics)
depends_on: context/sequential-operations.json (Phase A establishes sequential consumer op infrastructure)
session_startup: Run git status - verify on feat/sequential-operations-* branch
Verify Phase A is archived (context/completed/sequential-operations.json exists)
Read context/current-task.json and this plan file
Read the updated specs (Phase 0 output) to understand the repo-in-sequence rule
Find first task where status=pending and blocked_by=null

what_changes: How repo: operations are identified and processed in Phase 1 discovery
How the orchestrator drives the pipeline when repo: appears mid-sequence
Phase 2 processing: repo: is currently a no-op; must trigger inline sub-pipeline execution
Phase 5: may need to coordinate with mid-sequence repo resolution
what_stays_the_same: Phase A's apply_consumer_operations in Phase 5 (still needed for consumer ops that sandwich repo: calls)
Auto-merge semantics at the operator level

tasks.name: Read final spec to understand repo-in-sequence rule
tasks.description: Phase 0 produced the Q1 decision on how repo: resolves mid-sequence. Understand it fully before coding.
steps: Read spec/detailed/auto-merge-composition.allium for updated MergeLocalFiles and repo: resolution rules
Write a short trace (on paper or in a temp file) of how the bug scenario's self: block should execute under the spec
acceptance_criteria: Agent can state the Q1 decision in one sentence
Agent can describe what 'current FS state' means when repo: appears mid-sequence

tasks.name: Write E2E test reproducing the concrete bug scenario
tasks.description: Write an E2E test that reproduces the exact scenario from context/prompt.md — self: block with include/template/rename before repo: whose auto-merge must target a file renamed by the preceding ops. This test fails today and proves the architectural gap.
steps: Create or extend tests/cli_e2e_sequential_ops.rs (or new tests/cli_e2e_repo_in_sequence.rs)
Use init_test_git_repo helper pattern from tests/cli_e2e_defer.rs:12-56
Set up upstream repo with a deferred auto-merge (pre-commit-config.yaml with append_unique)
Consumer config: self: block with include src/** → template → rename src/(.*)$ → $1 → repo: <upstream>
Consumer working dir has src/.pre-commit-config.yaml with local content
Assert: final .pre-commit-config.yaml contains BOTH local and upstream hooks (array_mode append_unique merged)
Expected: FAIL - current code eagerly resolves repo: in Phase 1, not at declaration position
acceptance_criteria: E2E test exists and FAILS with current code
Test exercises the full scenario from context/prompt.md
Test will PASS after repo-in-sequence is implemented

tasks.name: Design the inline repo: resolution mechanism
tasks.description: PLACEHOLDER - detailed task will be written in Phase 0's flesh-out-phase-b-plan step. Options include: (a) pre-fetch all repos in Phase 1 but defer processing to the point in sequence where repo: appears; (b) recursively invoke the pipeline when processing hits a repo: op; (c) refactor orchestrator to walk ops as a tree with nested pipelines.
steps: TBD after Phase 0 produces the Q1 spec rule and flesh-out-phase-b-plan task writes concrete design options
acceptance_criteria: Design approach selected with trade-offs documented
Design matches the spec's Q1 decision

tasks.name: Implement the selected design
tasks.description: PLACEHOLDER - will be broken into sub-tasks after design is finalized. Likely needs sub-plan (plan-to-plan pattern) due to size.
acceptance_criteria: Inline repo: resolution works per the selected design
The failing E2E test now passes

tasks.name: Review, verify, and prepare for PR
tasks.description: PLACEHOLDER - will include code review agent dispatch, allium:weed to verify spec-code alignment, and full CI run.
acceptance_criteria: Code reviewer agent confirms implementation matches spec

notes: This plan is a SKELETON. Phase 0's flesh-out-phase-b-plan task converts it into a full plan after Q1 is decided.
Expect this plan to grow into sub-plans (plan-to-plan pattern per CLAUDE.md) — the architectural refactor is likely too large for a single flat plan.
The failing E2E test (write-failing-e2e-test) is the load-bearing acceptance criterion — it's the 'did we actually fix the reported bug' check.
Phase A's single-pass consumer ops are a prerequisite; confirm Phase A is merged before starting Phase B implementation work.
If design exploration reveals that Phase B needs Phase A's foundation to be changed, coordinate back through the plans rather than hacking Phase A retroactively.
